title: xanthophyllum eurhynchum subsp. maingayi
intro: Xanthophyllum eurhynchum subsp. maingayi, also known as Maingay's xanthophyllum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polygalaceae. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asian cultures, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, and has been shown to exhibit potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and ointments.
